element_introduction_order.mastery_context: Ragnar learns through simple battles with Healslime ally
Healie joins as healing companion, establishing early party dynamics
Multiple dungeon types traversed across chapters
Party members learn class-specific spells progressively
Three key tiers unlock progression gating
Unlocks sea-based encounters and distant locations
Highest mobility mechanic, enables endgame exploration
Hero can select active party members, party becomes tactical AI-controlled NPCs
Player assigns damage/healing/MP conservation priorities to party
Optional reward farming for special items via tokens
Found in drawers/jars, traded to secluded king for unique items
Environmental variation, some NPCs/encounters vary by time
Separated save and EXP tracking functions from king interaction

typical_relief_triggers: Town discovery with shops and healing services
Obtaining higher-tier keys to unlock new areas
typical_tension_triggers: Mandatory boss encounters at chapter climaxes
Resource scarcity (MP, healing items before towns)
Transition between overworld and enclosed spaces
Tactical party management demands in endgame
rhythm_pattern.pacing_descriptor: Chapter-based structure creates episodic tension cycles; each chapter builds toward climactic encounter then resets perspective. Chapter 5 convergence creates extended tension as all party members unite for world-threat narrative. Mid-game spike when ship travel introduces new monster zones; late-game tension sustained through complex party tactics and endgame dungeon crawls.

spatial_grammar.optional_content_signal: Small Medals hidden in drawers/jars serve as optional collectible signal; casino and mini-games optional side content; multiple dungeon entrances allow flexible progression within each chapter
spatial_grammar.secret_convention: Searchable furniture (drawers, jars) contains hidden items; secluded king in remote location rewards medal collection with rare items; world map contains travel doors enabling long-distance shortcuts

difficulty_curve.early_game: Chapters 1-2 introduce mechanics gradually with Ragnar and early party members; encounters designed as tutorials for combat and dungeon navigation; safe introduction to magic and items
difficulty_curve.mid_game: Chapters 3-4 expand party size and tactical complexity; new character perspectives add mechanical variety; ship travel introduces sea-exclusive monsters; difficulty increases with multi-stage dungeons
difficulty_curve.late_game: Chapter 5 convergence: Hero reunites all companions; Tactics AI system becomes essential; endgame dungeons demand careful party composition; final boss encounters spike difficulty significantly; mandatory use of wagon system increases strategic depth
notable_spikes: Chapter 1 climax: first mandatory boss encounter establishes combat expectation
Transition to Chapter 5: sudden shift to Hero-controlled party with all members as tactical NPCs
Ship acquisition: ocean encounters include unused sea enemy encounter bug (mitigated in remakes)
Final dungeon approach: endgame navigation requires balloon, multiple key types, and advanced party tactics

notable_level_patterns.description: Five distinct narrative chapters (six in remakes), each focused on different protagonist(s). Chapters 1-4 are companion backstories; Chapter 5 is Hero's main quest unifying all characters. Each chapter features introduction to 1-2 new party members, completion of local story arc, then narrative reset for next chapter perspective.
notable_level_patterns.example_level: Chapter 1: Ragnar's story in Burland; Chapter 5: Hero's convergence and world-threat finale

notable_level_patterns.description: Three-tier key system (Thief/Magic/Ultimate) controls dungeon and location access. Keys signal progression readiness; dungeons contain encounters scaled to intended key tier. Spatial gating tied directly to party power level.
notable_level_patterns.example_level: Early dungeons require Thief keys; late-game Ultimate-locked areas protect endgame content

notable_level_patterns.description: Ground→Ship→Balloon progression unlocks distinct world regions. Ship enables ocean navigation and sea encounters (with encounter bug limiting access to some zones in NES version). Balloon enables highest-altitude exploration of final areas.
notable_level_patterns.example_level: Shrine of Breaking Waves serves as transition point from ground to ship; balloon required for endgame castle approach

notable_level_patterns.description: Each chapter introduces 1-2 recruitable companions with unique abilities and story roles. Chapter 5 aggregates all companions into active wagon system with AI tactics. Party composition becomes strategic lever for difficulty tuning.
notable_level_patterns.example_level: Chapter 1: Ragnar+Healie; Chapter 5: Full party requires tactical assignment

notable_level_patterns.description: Casino appears as optional location offering slot machine, poker, and Monster Betting. Provides alternative progression path via token farming for rare items. Reward-based rather than narrative-critical.
notable_level_patterns.example_level: Casino accessible once major travel unlocks; Small Medals tradeable at secluded king location
